questions.q: What is RoxTrack?
questions.a: RoxTrack is a Garmin Connect IQ app designed specifically for HYROX races. It tracks your time, heart rate, and distance across all 8 running segments and 8 workout stations, giving you real-time performance data on your wrist.

questions.q: Which Garmin devices are supported?
questions.a: RoxTrack supports a wide range of Garmin watches — from the Fenix 6 series all the way through the latest Fenix 8, Forerunner 970, and Venu 4. It also works on touchscreen watches like the Vivoactive 5/6, Venu Sq 2, and Forerunner 165. In total, 50+ devices are supported including the Epix 2, Forerunner 245/255/265/745/945/955/965, and more. Check the Connect IQ store listing for the full compatibility list.

questions.q: Is RoxTrack free?
questions.a: Yes, RoxTrack is completely free! If you find it useful and want to support ongoing development, you can buy me a coffee. Donations are always appreciated but never required.

questions.q: How do I advance to the next segment?
questions.a: Press the Lap button on your watch to advance to the next segment. RoxTrack uses manual progression only — there's no auto-lap — so you're always in control of when transitions happen.

questions.q: What if I press Lap too early?
questions.a: No worries! Press the Down button to trigger the undo flow, which will reverse you back to the previous segment. Your time and distance are rewound so your data stays accurate.

questions.q: What data is shown during running segments?
questions.a: During running segments, RoxTrack displays your current pace, elapsed distance for that segment, segment time, and heart rate.

questions.q: What data is shown during workout stations?
questions.a: During station segments, you'll see the station name, the target reps or meters, your elapsed time for that station, and your heart rate.

questions.q: Where can I see my race history?
questions.a: RoxTrack saves your last 10 races on-device. You can access them from the History menu within the app. Each entry shows your total time and per-segment breakdowns.

questions.q: Does RoxTrack save to Garmin Connect?
questions.a: Yes! Each race is saved as a standard FIT activity file, which syncs automatically to Garmin Connect. You'll see individual laps for each segment in your activity details.

questions.q: Can I export my race data?
questions.a: Your race data is saved as a FIT file in Garmin Connect, which you can export and analyze in any compatible platform like Strava, TrainingPeaks, or WKO.

questions.q: Can I create custom workouts?
questions.a: Yes! As of v4, RoxTrack supports custom workout creation directly on the watch. You can build your own interval structures, give them a custom name, and save them for reuse. Please note that custom workouts are not supported on all devices — older or lower-memory watches may not have this feature available.

questions.q: Can I name my custom workouts?
questions.a: Yes! When creating a custom workout, you can give it a unique name using the on-watch text picker. This makes it easy to identify and select the right workout from your saved list.

questions.q: Why can't I see the custom workout option on my watch?
questions.a: Custom workouts require additional memory and are not available on all supported devices. Some older or entry-level watches may not have enough resources to run this feature. If you don't see the option, your device may not be compatible with custom workouts — but the core HYROX race tracking works on all supported watches.

questions.q: How many custom workouts can I save?
questions.a: You can save multiple custom workouts on your device. The exact limit depends on your watch's available storage, but most users can comfortably store 10+ workouts.

questions.q: Where do I find the settings menu?
questions.a: From the main screen, press the menu button (or tap on touchscreen watches) to open the settings menu. From here you can configure all race options before starting your warmup.

questions.q: What does Race Distance do?
questions.a: Race Distance lets you scale the workout to 25%, 50%, 75%, or 100% of a full HYROX race. All station targets and run distances adjust proportionally. This is great for training sessions where you want to practice the format without doing the full distance.

questions.q: How do I set a Run Pace Target?
questions.a: Select Run Pace Target from the settings menu and scroll to choose your target pace, from 3:30/km up to 8:00/km in 5-second increments. During the race, your pace display will be color-coded — green when you're on or ahead of your target, red when you're slower.

questions.q: What does Goal Time do?
questions.a: Goal Time lets you set a target finish time for your race, from 1 minute up to 3 hours. During the race, the timer will be color-coded: green when you're ahead of pace, orange when you're close (90%+), and red when you've exceeded your goal. Set it to OFF if you don't want goal tracking.

questions.q: What are the Run Mode options?
questions.a: Run Mode controls how RoxTrack tracks your running segments. Outdoor uses GPS for real distance and pace. Indoor disables GPS and estimates distance. Treadmill is optimized for treadmill running. Cardio is for elliptical or similar machines where distance and pace aren't available — run segments display like stations instead.

questions.q: What is the RoxZone setting?
questions.a: RoxZone adds transition segments between your runs and stations, just like the actual RoxZone areas in a HYROX race. When ON, these segments are tracked separately so you can see how long your transitions take. Turn it OFF if you want to go directly from station to run without tracking transitions.

questions.q: What does the Touch setting do?
questions.a: Touch is OFF by default to prevent accidental screen taps during a race. When you turn it ON, tapping the screen works the same as pressing the Lap button to advance to the next segment. This can be useful on touchscreen watches if you prefer tapping over pressing a physical button.

questions.q: The app crashed during my race. Is my data lost?
questions.a: If a crash occurs, any laps already recorded will be saved in the FIT file up to that point. The current in-progress segment may be lost. We recommend ensuring your watch firmware and the app are both up to date.

questions.q: My heart rate data looks wrong. What can I do?
questions.a: Heart rate accuracy depends on your watch sensor. For best results during HYROX, wear the watch snugly above the wrist bone. For maximum accuracy, consider pairing an external chest strap HR monitor via ANT+ or Bluetooth.

questions.q: How do I update RoxTrack?
questions.a: Updates are delivered through the Garmin Connect IQ Store. Open the Connect IQ app on your phone, go to My Apps, and check for available updates. We recommend keeping auto-updates enabled.
